demos.report_text: EXAMINATION:  CTA STROKE HEAD W WO CONTRAST CLINICAL HISTORY:  Neuro deficit  acute  stroke suspected COMPARISON:  None. TECHNIQUE: Head CTA with multi-planar MIP and volumetric rendering (3D) after bolus intravenous iodinated contrast administration was performed. All CT images were acquired using low-dose technique with automated exposure control. CT angiography data was processed using artificial intelligence software to assist stroke clinical decision making with regards to the diagnosis of large vessel occlusion. FINDINGS: There is a occlusive thrombus in the distal M1 segment of the right MCA with sluggish perfusion in the right MCA territory. The ACAs are patent. There is mild atherosclerotic narrowing in the M1 segment of the left MCA. The ICAs are patent. There is normal contrast enhancement with no significant stenosis or occlusion along bilateral vertebral arteries, basilar artery, cerebellar arteries, and PCAs. The posterior communicating arteries are not well visualized. There is no evidence of cerebral aneurysm in the proximal circle of Willis. There is normal contrast enhancement of major intracranial venous structures. IMPRESSION:  Occlusive thrombus in the distal M1 segment of the right MCA with sluggish flow in the right MCA territory. Findings discussed with  Adams, Taj Cordell, MD on 3/3/2024 7:02 AM , and he verbalized understanding of the report.   HMRM-MPHYMMR2

EXAMINATION:  CTA STROKE HEAD W WO CONTRAST CLINICAL HISTORY:  Neuro deficit  acute  stroke suspected COMPARISON:  None. TECHNIQUE: Head CTA with multi-planar MIP and volumetric rendering (3D) after bolus intravenous iodinated contrast administration was performed. All CT images were acquired using low-dose technique with automated exposure control. CT angiography data was processed using artificial intelligence software to assist stroke clinical decision making with regards to the diagnosis of large vessel occlusion.  IMPRESSION:  Occlusive thrombus in the M1 and M2 segments of the left MCA with the subtle cortical leptomeningeal collaterals. The ICAs ACAs and right MCA are patent. The posterior circulation is unremarkable. The posterior commuting arteries are not visualized. There is no definite aneurysm or vascular malformation. Findings discussed with  Shokraneh-Kenari, Keyon, MD  on 12/18/2024 7:56 PM , and the verbalized understanding of the report. 1RM1RADPS82

EXAMINATION: CT ANGIOGRAM HEAD W WO CONTRAST   CLINICAL HISTORY: Neuro deficit  acute  stroke suspected    COMPARISON:  None    TECHNIQUE:  Imaging of the intracranial circulation was obtained from the skull base to the vertex during the arterial phase of enhancement. MIP multiplanar and 3D reconstructed images were performed on a separate workstation.      FINDINGS:    No hemodynamically significant stenosis is identified of the intracranial internal carotid arteries, middle cerebral arteries, anterior cerebral arteries, intracranial vertebral arteries, basilar artery or posterior cerebral arteries. There is no  aneurysmal dilatation or vascular malformation of the circle of Willis.    IMPRESSION:    No hemodynamically significant narrowing of the circle of Willis vessels.      HMH-2UA6442QNM

EXAMINATION:  CTA STROKE HEAD W WO CONTRAST CLINICAL HISTORY:  Neuro deficit  acute  stroke suspected COMPARISON:  None. TECHNIQUE: CT imaging was performed with iterative reconstruction technique and/or automated exposure control to reduce radiation dose. Study was obtained during the arterial phase of enhancement.   IMPRESSION:  3-D reconstructions were processed off-line. Occlusion of the M1 segment of left MCA compatible with large vessel occlusion. Severe left and moderate right intracranial vertebral artery narrowing. Moderate narrowing of the mid basilar artery. No other focal narrowing, aneurysmal dilatation or vascular malformation of the circle of Willis vessels. Findings were discussed with and acknowledged by DANIEL PATRICK KELLY at 9/24/2024 6:41 PM.  HMRM-WPHYRRS

signature.instructions: Report Text should prompt the Language Model to extract information about acute stroke-related vascular territory from CTA report text, specifically focusing on the location of occlusive thrombus, stenosis, or hemodynamically significant narrowing in the intracranial circulation.

fields.description: Only comma-separated labels from the allowed list. Example: RMCA or NONE.
Exactly one short sentence. No step-by-step reasoning.
